note: Menu Link Attributes adds a configurable Attributes fieldset to the menu link content form. All cases are `recipe` (response-graded), grounded in the module docs; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with the Menu Link Attributes module, where do I control which HTML attributes editors can set on menu links? Name the configuration object and the admin path.
evals.expected_output: Edit the single config object menu_link_attributes.config (key `attributes`) at /admin/config/menu_link_attributes/config (Structure -> Menus -> Available attributes), a YAML textarea.

evals.prompt: Which menu link attributes does the Menu Link Attributes module make available out of the box, before I customize anything?
evals.expected_output: Out of the box it ships container_class, class, and target.

evals.prompt: Using Menu Link Attributes, I want a CSS class applied to the menu item's <li> wrapper element rather than the <a> link. How does the module decide whether an attribute goes on the <li> container vs the <a>?
evals.expected_output: Attributes whose name starts with container_ (e.g. container_class) or that set `container: true` are applied to the <li> container; other attributes render on the <a>.

evals.prompt: With Menu Link Attributes, where are the attribute values I set on a menu link actually stored on the entity, and how do they end up on the rendered <a> element?
evals.expected_output: Values are stored in the menu link's `link` field options under the `attributes` (link) and `container_attributes` (container) keys, and render through core menu/link theming.

evals.prompt: In PHP on a Drupal 11 site, set the class `btn btn-primary` on the <a> of an existing menu_link_content link programmatically (Menu Link Attributes is enabled). Show the code.
evals.expected_output: Write into $menu_link->link->first()->options: set $options['attributes']['class'] = ['btn', 'btn-primary'] (or container_attributes for the <li>), assign back and call $menu_link->save().

evals.prompt: In the Menu Link Attributes YAML config, I want to add a `target` attribute that editors pick from a dropdown of preset values (_blank / _self) with a default. What sub-keys do I define for that attribute?
evals.expected_output: Define the attribute with `type: select`, an `options` map of value: label pairs (e.g. _blank / _self), and optionally `default_value`; also `label`/`description`.

evals.prompt: Menu Link Attributes: which permission lets a role set attribute values on the menu link edit form, and which separate permission lets a role define the list of available attributes?
evals.expected_output: `use menu link attributes` gates setting values on the link form; `administer menu link attributes` (restricted) gates the available-attributes config form.
